title: Modern Slavery Act 2015 §54 — Transparency in supply chains
text: Commercial organisations with global turnover ≥ £36m must publish an annual slavery and human trafficking statement. Statement must describe: organisation structure, supply chains, policies, due diligence processes, risk assessment, KPIs, training. Approved by the board, signed by a director, published on the organisation's website.

title: Germany LkSG (Lieferkettensorgfaltspflichtengesetz)
text: Companies > 1000 employees (since 2024) must conduct human-rights and environmental due diligence covering own business + tier-1 suppliers. Risk-based DD covers 11 protected legal positions including: forced labour, child labour, occupational safety, freedom of association, discrimination, fair wages. Environmental risks include: mercury, persistent organic pollutants, hazardous waste exports.

title: France Loi sur le Devoir de Vigilance (2017)
text: Companies > 5000 employees in France (or > 10000 worldwide) must establish a vigilance plan covering the whole value chain. Plan must identify risks of serious infringements of human rights, health, safety, environment caused by the company, controlled companies, and suppliers/subcontractors with established commercial relationships. Plan must be effectively implemented and published in the annual report.

title: Norway Åpenhetsloven (2022)
text: Companies above thresholds (revenue NOK 70M / balance NOK 35M / 50 employees) must conduct DD on fundamental human rights and decent working conditions in their own operations + supply chain + business partners. Companies must publish an annual account of DD findings + responses by 30 June each year, and respond to information requests from the public within 3 weeks.

title: Switzerland Code of Obligations Art. 964j (Responsible Business Initiative ordinance)
text: Public-interest companies must publish annual non-financial report covering: environmental matters, social matters, employees, respect for human rights, anti-corruption. Specific DD required for child-labour and conflict-minerals (tin/tantalum/tungsten/gold) under separate ordinance.

title: US Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act (2021)
text: Rebuttable presumption that any goods, wares, articles, or merchandise mined, produced, or manufactured wholly or in part in Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region of China, or by entities on the UFLPA Entity List, are made with forced labour and therefore prohibited from importation under 19 USC §1307. Importers must produce 'clear and convincing evidence' to rebut.

title: US Tariff Act §307 (19 USC §1307)
text: Goods produced wholly or in part by forced labour, indentured labour, or prison labour are prohibited from entry into the United States. CBP issues Withhold Release Orders against shipments believed to be in violation; importers may rebut. WROs escalate to Findings if forced-labour is confirmed.

title: Canada Bill S-211 (Fighting Against Forced Labour and Child Labour in Supply Chains Act, 2024)
text: Entities listed on a Canadian stock exchange OR doing business in Canada above thresholds must publish an annual report on steps taken to prevent and reduce the risk of forced labour or child labour at any step of producing/manufacturing/distributing goods. Report due by 31 May annually.

title: Australia Modern Slavery Act (2018)
text: Entities based or operating in Australia with consolidated revenue ≥ AUD 100M must publish an annual Modern Slavery Statement. Statement must address 7 mandatory criteria including operations, supply-chain structure, risks of modern slavery, actions taken, effectiveness, consultation, other relevant information.

title: Japan METI Guidelines on Respecting Human Rights (2022)
text: Non-binding guidelines requiring large Japanese companies to commit to respecting human rights, conduct human rights due diligence, and provide for remediation. Aligned to the UN Guiding Principles. Covers own operations and supply chain partners.

title: California Transparency in Supply Chains Act (SB 657, 2010)
text: Retail sellers and manufacturers doing business in California with global revenue > $100M must disclose efforts to eradicate slavery and human trafficking from direct supply chains. 5 areas: verification, audits, certification, internal accountability, training. Disclosure on company website.

title: Netherlands Child Labour Due Diligence Law
text: Companies selling goods or services to Dutch end-users must investigate whether the goods/services they provide have been produced using child labour, and develop a plan of action to address found cases. Statement filed with Dutch supervisory authority.
